qa.question: What should I do if a child is choking?
qa.answer: Give 5 back slaps, then 5 chest thrusts. If unconscious, start CPR.

qa.question: Kufanele ngenze njani uma umntwana ekhala?
qa.answer: Yenza i-back slaps ezi-5, bese i-chest thrusts ezi-5. Uma enganyakazi, qala i-CPR.

qa.question: Ke lokela ho etsa joang ha ngoana a khang?
qa.answer: Etsa li-back slaps tse 5, ebe li-chest thrusts tse 5. Ha a sa tsitsa, qala CPR.

qa.question: Should I remove the object from the wound?
qa.answer: No. Apply pressure around the object. Do not remove it.

qa.question: Kufanele ngikhiphe into esikhenkqeni?
qa.answer: Cha. Cindezela ema-circumstances around the object. Ungasusi into esikhenkqeni.

qa.question: Ke lokela ho tlosa ntho leqebeng?
qa.answer: Che. Hatella ho potoloha ntho. U se ke oa e tlosa.

qa.question: Should I put butter on a burn?
qa.answer: No. Use cool water for 20 minutes. Never apply butter or oil.

qa.question: Kufanele ngifake i-butter emzimbeni?
qa.answer: Cha. Sebenzisa amanzi apholile imizuzu engama-20. Ungafaki i-butter noma i-oil.

qa.question: Ke lokela ho tšela seretse leqebeng?
qa.answer: Che. Sebelisa metsi a pholileng metsotso e 20. U se ke oa tšela seretse kapa oli.

qa.question: How fast should I do CPR compressions?
qa.answer: 100 to 120 per minute. Press 5-6 cm deep.

qa.question: Kufanele ngicindezele kanjani i-CPR?
qa.answer: Cindezela phakathi kwe-100 no-120 ngomzuzu. Cindezela ubude be-5-6 cm.

qa.question: Ke lokela ho hatella CPR ka lebelo lefe?
qa.answer: 100 ho isa 120 ka motsotso. Hatella botebo ba 5-6 cm.

qa.question: Should I put something in their mouth?
qa.answer: No. Never put anything in their mouth. Turn them on their side.

qa.question: Kufanele ngifake into emlonyeni womuntu?
qa.answer: Cha. Ungafaki lutho emlonyeni. Muphendulele eceleni.

qa.question: Ke lokela ho kenya ntho ka hanong?
qa.answer: Che. U se ke oa kenya letho ka hanong. Mo fetole ka lehlakoreng.

qa.question: What should I do for an allergic reaction?
qa.answer: Use an EpiPen if available. Call emergency services immediately.

qa.question: Kufanele ngenze njani uma umuntu engena i-allergic reaction?
qa.answer: Sebenzisa i-EpiPen uma enayo. Shayela i-10177 ngokushesha.

qa.question: Ke lokela ho etsa joang ha motho a e-na le allergic reaction?
qa.answer: Sebelisa EpiPen ha e le teng. Letsetsa litšebeletso tsa tšohanyetso hang.

qa.question: What should I do for an asthma attack?
qa.answer: Use their inhaler. Sit them down, leaning forward.

qa.question: Kufanele ngenze njani uma umuntu engena i-asthma?
qa.answer: Sebenzisa i-inhaler yakhe. Mhlale phansi, ame ngomqolo.

qa.question: Ke lokela ho etsa joang ha motho a e-na le asthma?
qa.answer: Sebelisa inhaler ea hae. Mo lulise fatše, a sekame pele.

qa.question: How do I check for a stroke?
qa.answer: Use FAST: Face, Arm, Speech, Time.

qa.question: Kufanele ngihlole kanjani i-stroke?
qa.answer: Sebenzisa i-FAST: Face (umunwe), Arm (ingalo), Speech (inkulumo), Time (isikhathi).

qa.question: Ke lokela ho hloka stroke joang?
qa.answer: Sebelisa FAST: Face (sefahleho), Arm (letsoho), Speech (puo), Time (nako).

qa.question: Should I try to straighten the bone?
qa.answer: No. Splint it in place. Do not move it.

qa.question: Kufanele ngilungise i-bone?
qa.answer: Cha. Ungayilungisi. Yibophe ngendwangu, umise esikhundleni.

qa.question: Ke lokela ho leka ho otlolla lesapo?
qa.answer: Che. Le fanyehe sebakeng sa lona. U se ke oa le sisinya.

qa.question: What should I do for labour?
qa.answer: Lay them down in a clean area. Call emergency services.

qa.question: Kufanele ngenze njani uma umuntu eza i-labour?
qa.answer: Mthole phansi, mthole endaweni epholile. Shayela i-10177.

qa.question: Ke lokela ho etsa joang ha motho a le beleheng?
qa.answer: Mo robale fatše sebakeng se hloekileng. Letsetsa litšebeletso tsa tšohanyetso.
